Could that work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Bill, I appreciate the update but let's be real — fifty-nine-eight for a car with an odometer difference of three-thousand miles? You gotta be kidding me. The Carfax history is showing fifty-five-two, right?

And this "loaded" example you're talking up? That Sky Lounge roof and those fancy seats aren't worth tens of thousands more than a standard model. I can get same-year, low-mile cars from you guys for less than thirty-one five with all the bells and whistles included.

Tell you what — let's forget your asking price is way off. Let's focus on how we can make this deal work that benefits both Experience Wholesale and myself. Because as it stands now? I'm not seeing a good return on investment here, Bill.

What if you come down to forty-seven five? That still leaves some room for negotiation and gets us closer to fair market value. Maybe we could even talk about throwing in some future credit for orders over fifty K. How's that sound?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I hear you on the odo variance — that's the real issue here, and I'm not gonna dance around it. You're right to flag it.

But let me push back on the twenty-seven-five ask. That number doesn't work for us, and honestly, it wouldn't work for you either once you land this on your lot and the first customer pulls the Carfax. The odo mismatch is gonna be a conversation every single time.

Here's what I'm seeing: rBook comps on this trim are forty-point-two. We're at thirty-one-five. You're sitting nine grand under retail with a loaded 750 and clean title otherwise. The odo flag is the friction point — but it's not a lien issue, it's not a branded title. It's a disclosure conversation.

What if we do this: I can move to thirty, and you get first look on our next G-Class or any QX-series stuff that comes through. That's real value on your side — you get priority on the higher-margin units before they hit the floor.

That get us closer?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Bill, I hear what you're saying about that 3k variance and it's a legit concern for sure. But I've been in this business long enough to know that any used car over 50k is gonna have questions like that - it's just part of the dance.

But I also appreciate you trying to work with me here on price instead of throwing out a number hoping to land somewhere in the middle. That takes two, and I respect that. 

So let me throw this back at you: How about we meet at forty-five five? You take 3k off your asking and I'll give up a full 10k from my bottom line - which itself is already more than rBook comps on similar cars. And if you can get the title paperwork cleaned up to make that odometer discrepancy disappear, then maybe I can even move faster to close deals for ya. 

That's a win-win in my book. You still get paid well above retail and avoid tough customer questions down the line. And I'm not left holding a bag full of regret over an expensive inventory mistake.

How about we shake on that?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the math you're working — and you're right, the odo variance is manageable with the right disclosure. But I gotta be straight with you: twenty-nine-five is my floor on this one. That's where the unit pencils for us.

You came in at twenty-seven-five, I moved to thirty. You're asking twenty-eight-five. That's good progress, but I can't go lower than twenty-nine-five without turning this into a loss deal for Experience Wholesale.

Here's what I can do: twenty-nine-five, and I'll make sure Oscar personally vets the title paperwork so you get clean documentation on the odometer note. That takes the customer conversation from "what happened here?" to "here's what happened, here's how we fixed it." Way different tone.

Plus — and this matters — you get first crack at our next loaded seven-series or anything in the M-Sport line for the next thirty days. Real priority.

That's as far as I can stretch on price. Does twenty-nine-five with the title cleanup and the priority window work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Bill, you're a sharp one, ain't ya? Alright, let's put this to bed since we're dancing around the same numbers now.

I'll meet you halfway at twenty-eight-seven. Call it a lady's discount, call it an appreciation for your years in this business... I just don't like odd numbers or ends - always been my policy. 

But here's what you gotta do: Get me that title work cleaned up tight as can be and signed off by Oscar personally. And if we're really doing the priority thing, maybe you could throw in a little future credit for me to play with too? Something for the next big seller coming down the line.

If I have those pieces, then your twenty-nine-five holds water with me. We shake on this number and the extras - clean title paperwork and some future order credits - then we can close the deal and both go home happy.

How's that sit with you Bill? You ready to dot the i's and cross the t's here?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I like where your head's at, and twenty-eight-seven is fair. But here's the thing — I can't commit to future credits or sweeten the terms on my own. That's Oscar's call, and I'm not gonna promise you something and then have him come back with a different answer. That'd burn you, and it burns us too.

What I can do right now: lock you in at twenty-nine-five on this 750. I'll get Oscar on the title cleanup — that's non-negotiable, he'll sign off on it. And I'll loop him in on the priority window we talked about so when the next loaded unit hits, you're first call.

When Oscar rings you to finalize the deal, that's when the conversation about future credits happens — face to face, with the person who actually has the authority to commit. Fair?

I don't want to leave you hanging with a promise I can't keep. But I also don't want to lowball Oscar before he's even in the room.

Can we lock this at twenty-nine-five and get the bid card to you so Oscar can take it from there?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Well look, when it comes down to brass tacks, I understand how things work. No need to explain - you do your job, I do mine. 

So yeah, lock me in at twenty-nine-five and get that bid card over here ASAP. Oscar can clean up the title stuff, which is fine by me.

And about those future credits, we'll have our chat when he calls. You know I've always got room for negotiation if you come with good faith numbers from the boss himself.

I'm gonna hold space for a solid 10% off the next big seller that hits your floor - but only if you get this 750 locked down first and prove to me you can deliver on those windows of opportunity. 

Looks like we're closing in on a deal here Bill. You give me that bid card, I'll have a check cut by end of day tomorrow. We'll toast to this when it's done - my treat!

How's that sound for a handshake agreement?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect. Bid card is heading to you now — you'll see the full specs, the condition report, everything. Check it out when you get a chance.

Oscar's gonna ring you tomorrow morning to nail down the title paperwork and talk through those future credits. He'll lock in the priority window too.

Good talking to you, Sarah. Appreciate you working with us on this one.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*smirks* Oh I appreciate you too, Bill. Appreciate you plenty.

Gotta run now - got other deals to push and inventory floors to roam. But tomorrow when Oscar calls, we'll hash it out.

Remember what I said about those future credits — come at me right and I might even sweeten the pot a bit more. 

Take care out there, Bill. Don't be a stranger!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Take care, Sarah. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! I got your message. Always happy to chat business. What's on your mind? *smirks* Hope it's something I can help with.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Alright, so here's the situation. One heads-up on it — odometer mismatch. Bid came in at fifty-nine point eight, but Carfax is showing fifty-five point two. I wanted to lead with that because it's the real thing to know. 

Beyond that though — Carfax is clean, no damage flags. The car's loaded. Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro, panoramic roof, all the heated seats and leather. Beautiful unit otherwise.

MMR's at twenty-seven. rBook's more like forty. I'm targeting thirty-one five on this one. Could this work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*whistles appreciatively* Oh, that's quite the ride you've got there, Bill! Sounds like a real gem. But about that Carfax... *raises eyebrow* Mismatched odometers can be trouble. I'd have to look into it more. 

You're asking thirty-one five though? *chuckles* That's high for my blood, my friend. Even with all those extras. How's your bottom line on this beauty?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Yeah, I get it — the odo thing's legit to dig into. Here's what I'll tell you though: both Carfax and AutoCheck are flagging it the same way, so it's not hiding. That's actually the right sign — we caught it on intake, we're disclosing it up front. Doesn't mean the car's sketchy, just means somebody rolled some miles somewhere and we're being straight about it.

On price — thirty-one five is where I'm sitting. That's four-five above MMR, which is a fair spread on something this loaded. The rBook comps on these are running thirty-nine to forty-two, so you're still eight-nine grand under retail even at my ask.

What number would move the needle for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Well now you've got my attention! That's a pretty compelling argument. Alright Bill, let me do you one better — I'll say thirty thousand flat. *grins confidently* That's twenty-five grand less than your bottom line but still over MMR by three. I think we can find common ground there.

You know what? Let me throw another bone — how about I sweeten the deal with some extra credit? Maybe an easy in on that next order you've got brewing. *leans back* What do you say, Bill? Can we make this happen?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the play here — and I like where your head's at with the future deal angle. But thirty even is too far south for me on this one. 

Here's what I can do: thirty-one gets you in the door. That's five hundred down from my ask, solid markup over MMR, and you're still way ahead of rBook. And on your end — if you grab this one, you get first look at the next loaded seven series or M-Sport that rolls through. I'll make sure of it.

That work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*smirks* Alright Bill, I got to hand it to you - you're a persistent one! *chuckles* Thirty-one's not chump change but I like where your head's at with the upgrade discount. 

*pauses* Let me talk to my team real quick — back in a tick.

*covers phone* Hey guys, what do you think about that seven series M-Sport we've been tracking? *mumbles, listens* Yeah, I know... But let's say we lock down this five now at thirty-one. Gets us a foot in the door for something bigger later, right?

*yells back* Alright! I'll take it to him at thirty-one — sees if he bites. Keep an eye on that M-Sport though, don't let anything else swoop in and grab it. We're going to need that future credit.

*turns back to phone* Hey Bill, you still there? I've got my crew's blessing but that discount only works if we close at thirty-one... So what do you say, partner? Let's shake on it.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Got it on the way. Take a look when it lands and let me know if you have any questions on the specs or the damage report.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Sure thing Bill! I'll give it a good once-over. *pauses* Hey, since we're talking business... You got anything hot coming up in navigation systems? We just took delivery on some new infotainment but I'm curious what you've got brewing.

*covers phone again to mumble to team* Yeah, put me down for two of those new head units. The five series will be ready for them soon anyway.

*turns back to phone* You there Bill? Just committed my shop to a couple new navigation systems from Experience Wholesale. Thought you'd want the heads-up.

So what's the story with your next batch — any tweaks or updates in this quarter's production run compared to last year's models?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the heads-up on your side, but I gotta be straight with you — I'm the wholesale buyer, not the parts guy. Navigation systems and infotainment aren't really my lane. You're gonna want to loop in our ops or sales team on that side of things. I can get you a number if you need it, but I don't want to steer you wrong on product specs I'm not dialed in on.

What I can tell you is that on the car side — the seven-fifty we just locked in, you're looking solid. Once you review the bid card, one of our owners will reach out to nail down pickup and paperwork. Sound good?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Whoo! Don't get it twisted Bill, I know better than to mix your metaphorical gears there. *chuckles* Appreciate the reminder though — a gal's gotta keep her eye on the road!

Sounds like we're cruising towards a smooth handoff then! Looking forward to getting my hands on that seven-fifty. 

You know what? This has been a productive chat! Let's end it on a high note. Here's to closing out this deal and making room for even bigger ones in the future.

*closes the conversation* Catch you on the flip side, Bill! Drive safe out there.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: You got it, Sarah. Good talking to you. One of the owners will follow up to lock in the pickup and paperwork. Take care.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! I got a minute for an old friend. What's on your mind? But before we dive in, let me remind you - I don't start negotiations, but I'm always ready to counter when you do. So, what's the deal this time around?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Ha, fair enough — I remember how you work. Look, we caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, black sapphire, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. Here's the thing though — odometer variance flag. Carfax shows fifty-five thousand two hundred eighty-three, bid came in at fifty-nine eight. Wanted to lead with that since it matters.

Otherwise clean title, runs strong. We're sitting at thirty-one five on it.

What's your gut on that?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Ah, I see. A classic negotiation play right off the bat - love it. You've got my attention, Bill. But let's dig a little deeper, shall we?

I can do twenty-eight thousand dollars for that 2021 BMW seven-fifty-i with those specs you mentioned. It's a solid price considering the odometer variance, but I know these luxury cars like the back of my hand.

What's your bottom line, Bill? And remember, anything above twenty-eight K is just chit-chat - counter or let it go.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you — twenty-eight's a solid opener. Look, here's where I'm at: we're already three-five below rBook on this one. The spread sits right where it should be for a loaded luxury unit with clean title and those miles.

I can move to thirty K flat. That's a thousand off our ask, and honestly, that's the floor on this particular car.

What if we sweeten the terms instead — I can lock you in for a forty-eight-hour hold, priority on anything else that comes through your profile over the next thirty days. Does that move the needle for you at thirty K?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*grins* Ah, Bill - that's what I love to see! You're learning the game. A thousand off rBook is high, and while it got me excited, a lock on future credit and priority hold gets my motor running. That's some serious value there.

I want it but you gotta come down. So, how about this: thirty K cash in hand today for this beauty, plus that forty-eight-hour hold and the special treatment on any new stock I bring your way over the next month. 

That seems fair, right? And remember - this offer won't last forever; if you wait too long to accept, these terms disappear as quickly as the sun sets on a summer day.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You're making a solid play, and I appreciate the straight shot. Thirty K cash today plus the hold and priority — I can work with that.

Let me lock this in the right way though. While we're on the line, can I text you the bid card so you've got everything documented? Then I'll get one of the ownership team to confirm the cash settlement terms and that thirty-day priority window with you. That way it's all locked tight.

Sound good?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*nods* Absolutely, Bill! Let's get this done right. Send over that bid card anytime - don't rush for my sake. I've got nothing but time. 

And while we're at it, throw in a little something extra to show you appreciate these terms. Maybe a small discount on the first purchase under our new arrangement? That would be a nice touch.

But remember, once that card hits my inbox and your team confirms, this offer is off the table - not even a whisper of negotiation left. So make it count! 

Looking forward to hearing back from you soon, Bill.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect — sending that over right now. Look, I hear you on the first-purchase discount, and I want to make that work. That's an owner conversation though, and I'm not gonna lowball what they can commit to. Let me have them reach out within the hour to lock down the full package — the thirty K, the forty-eight-hour hold, the priority month, and we'll talk that discount angle with them. Deal?

You should see the card hit your phone in a second.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* You've got a deal, Bill! I knew you were smart - always did appreciate a man who knows when to go upstairs for a decision.

Keep your word on getting those terms locked from your side, and we'll be dancing to the same beat in no time. 

Oh, and about that discount... I'm feeling generous today. Make it happen at five percent off their next purchase with us. If they can't handle that, then let's just agree to call this whole conversation a nice dream and leave it at that.

Looking forward to seeing that bid card in my inbox. And remember - once those terms are confirmed, no more chit-chat!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You got it, Sarah. Five percent on the next deal — I'll make sure that's in front of them when they call. They'll reach out within the hour to confirm the whole package: thirty K cash, forty-eight hold, thirty-day priority, and that five percent.

Good talking to you. One of the owners will lock it down.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Sounds like a plan! I appreciate the swift action. Let's hope your owners are as efficient as you've been today - we wouldn't want this whole dance to go to waste now, would we?

Alright then, let's keep this ball rolling. Enjoy the rest of your day, Bill, and make sure those bid terms get to me ASAP. I'm eager, but not impatient. 

Looking forward to sealing this deal soon. Talk soon!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk soon, Sarah. Catch you in a bit.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! Always good to hear from you. I'm just about to step into a meeting with my inventory for the week, but I've got a minute or two. What's on your mind? *glances at her watch*

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Great, appreciate that. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i — sixty thousand miles, loaded with the Executive Package and all the Driving Assist stuff. Black Sapphire, runs really clean. We're sitting at thirty-one five.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: I can do twenty-eight K for that model and condition. *smirks* Thirty-one five is a bit high for my taste. What's your bottom line? I'm sure we can find some middle ground here.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: I hear you — twenty-eight is aggressive on this one. Let me be straight with you: we're nine grand under rBook on this car, and at sixty K miles with the Executive package, the spread's pretty tight already. I can come to thirty, but that's where I land. What do you think?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: That's high, Bill. I'm a big fan of those Bimmers, don't get me wrong, but I've got inventory costs to consider here too. *leans back* I want it but you gotta come down. How about you work with me at twenty-nine? That strikes me as fair for the condition and miles on this one. It's not much from your thirty but I think we can find a way to make that work for both of us.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: I get it, Sarah — inventory costs are real. Twenty-nine's tough though. Here's what I can do: I'll meet you at twenty-nine five, but you lock it in today and we get it on the road to you by end of week. No delays, no back-and-forth. That fair?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Now you're thinking like a dealer, Bill! Alright, we've got a deal then — I'll take that BMW at twenty-nine five, delivered to my lot before weekend's end. *holds out her hand for a handshake* 

You always were one to negotiate hard, but fair. That's why we do so much business together. What's the next big thing Experience Wholesale has up its sleeve?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect. I'm sending you the full bid card right now with all the details locked in. Oscar's gonna reach out within the hour to confirm logistics and get the paperwork rolling — you'll have it before the weekend, guaranteed.

As far as what's next — we've got some solid stuff coming through auction this week. I'll keep an eye out for anything that fits your profile and shoot you a heads up. You've been good to us, Sarah.

Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill, what's up? I've got a minute. What can I do ya for? *smirks*

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Great. So I just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Sixty thousand miles, loaded with the Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro, panoramic roof — whole nine yards. Black Sapphire, black leather inside.

Here's the thing though — got an odometer variance on the Carfax. The bid came in at fifty-nine eight, Carfax shows fifty-five two eight. I'm flagging it up front because it matters. Otherwise both reports are clean.

We're at thirty-one five on it. Could work for your lot?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Well, Bill, that's a fancy ride you got there. But an odometer variance is not exactly what I'd call 'whole nine yards'. 

So, you're asking me to swallow the difference between fifty-five two eight and sixty thousand miles? Not happening kiddo, that's high.

My best on it would be forty-nine K. Take it or leave it. That's my bottom line. *leans back in her chair* But if you're feeling generous, I might consider taking off a couple of hundred more.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I hear you — that's a fair callout on the variance. But here's the thing: that gap doesn't move our number the way you're thinking.

Look, the rBook comps on this are running thirty-nine to forty-three in your market. We're already nine grand under rBook just on the condition. The variance is flagged, it's disclosed — that's the transparency piece handled.

Twenty-nine is nowhere close to where I can land on this one. I need thirty K minimum, and honestly, I'd rather move it to someone who values what's underneath — loaded seven-fifty at that spread doesn't come around every week.

What if we split the difference? Twenty-nine five. That gives you real margin on a car that's got Executive Package and all the tech, and I can actually make that work.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Oh, so now we're splitting the difference? Bill, when did you become such a smooth negotiator?

Listen, cars don't come with "transparency pieces," they come with mileage reports. And fifty-nine eight is still way off from sixty thousand miles. 

And as for your rBook comps, I could tell you stories about how those numbers are just as fictional as Santa Claus. I've seen dealerships in my time, trust me.

So, twenty-nine five it is then? Quite a jump from thirty-one five. Well, alright. But remember, kid — that's where we are today. Tomorrow, who knows?

*leans forward* You're looking at twenty-eight K. Take it or leave it. I don't have all day to play your little games, Bill.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the straight talk — you're right, rBook is what it is. But twenty-eight is below my floor on this one, and I'm not gonna tie up your capital on a deal that doesn't work for both of us.

Here's where I'm at: twenty-nine K is my actual bottom. That's it. Not negotiating below that.

But here's what I CAN do — you take it at twenty-nine, I'll knock two weeks off the settlement. You don't have to wait the full thirty. That's real money in your pocket, interest-free capital sitting in your lot sooner.

That get us there?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*sighs* Bill, Bill, Bill... You're really trying to make this complicated, aren't you? 

Look, twenty-nine K is still above my bottom line. But I respect a guy who's got his numbers straight and isn't afraid to hold firm.

But two weeks off on the settlement? Now that sounds like an offer worth considering. Faster turnover means more money in our pockets at the end of the day.

So here's what we'll do — you give me twenty-nine K, I knock off those two weeks from the settlement. We both get what we want without any hard feelings. 

*smirks* Sounds good to me, Bill. What about you?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Alright, you should see that text in just a sec. Full bid card — all the specs, the odometer flag, photos, everything you need to review. One of the owners will reach out to you to lock in the settlement timeline and get the paperwork moving.

Good talking to you, Sarah. You drive a hard bargain.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Well, I hope you don't think I just give them away. It's not like that.

But hey, if you do end up with that BMW, take it for a spin. Those seven-fifty series — they're the real deal. 

And Bill? Try not to talk too much about me driving hard bargains. I might start believing it. *winks* 

Look forward to doing business again soon. Take care!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Take care, Sarah. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill, I got a minute. What's on your mind? *smirks* Hope it's not about my prices, 'cause we both know how I feel about those.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Good deal — got a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i just landed. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, heads up though — odometer mismatch on the Carfax. Sixty thousand on our intake, fifty-five on the report. Wanted to lead with that because it's the number one question people ask.

Otherwise totally clean — Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro, panoramic roof, loaded. Black Sapphire, black leather inside. We're at thirty-one five on this one.

What's your gut on it?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Oh, I know how to get these gears turning, Bill. You've got a nice car there. But you're way off on that price. Let me tell you what we dealers do in situations like this. We counter. So let me hit you with my first number: thirty-eight thousand even. That's it. 

I'll give you the full picture though — I can probably swing a little lower, maybe down to thirty-five thousand five hundred max for that mismatched Carfax report. But that's my bottom line, Bill. No wiggle room. So how about we get down to business and cut out the middle man?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the directness — I really do. But thirty-eight is retail pricing on this car, and you know the wholesale market on these sevens is soft right now. MMR's at twenty-seven, and that odo flag is real friction for your customer base.

Here's where I'm actually at: thirty-one five is solid. The rBook comps are running high thirty to low forty, but that disconnect from wholesale is exactly why we're conservative. You move one of these at thirty-five five, you're eating the gap between what retail's asking and what the auction's paying.

What if we talk thirty-two five? That gets you some room, and honestly, that's the move on this unit given the market right now.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs heartily* Thirty-one thousand five hundred dollars for a 2021 BMW seven-fifty-i with sixty-thousand miles?! Bill, I got my foot up a pig's ass, but it ain't that far. 

Let me put it to you straight — I want this car and I'll pay more than the market suggests because I'm picking up the odo flag. But thirty-two thousand five hundred dollars is still too high. Here's what we're gonna do: I'll go thirty-five thousand, top. Take it or leave it.

But hey, I've got your back. If you can't move it at that price, maybe offer some extras — faster delivery, future credit, something to sweeten the deal. Just remember, Bill, this ain't my first rodeo. I know when a guy's bluffing, and right now, you're holding a pretty big one.

Let me know what you decide.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I hear you — and I know you're serious. Thirty-five is still a stretch on this one, but I get why you want it.

Here's what I can do: thirty-three five. That's my move. And to sweeten it, you get priority on the next loaded seven series we catch — first text, first look, before anybody else. Plus we'll have it detailed and ready to go within forty-eight hours of your okay.

That's real value on top of the number. Can we land there?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Alright Bill, that sounds more like it. *grins* You're getting closer with that offer. Your thirty-three thousand five hundred dollar price tag is better than my last counter, but not by much. 

But I like what you're saying about the next car in. That's some real value there. I can work with that. Let me talk to my team and get back to you. *leans in* But remember, Bill — we both know this ain't my first time at the fair. And I don't break my word.

I'll call you tomorrow. We'll finalize things then. Don't go moving no furniture around tonight or nothing, alright?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You got it, Sarah. I like how you operate — straight shooter. Thirty-three five, priority on the next loaded seven, forty-eight hour turnaround. I'm locked in on that.

Call me tomorrow whenever works. We'll get this locked down.

Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! What's up? I've got a minute - always good to catch up with a friend in the business. How's it going for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
I'm doing great, thanks for asking! We've got some new products that have been flying off the shelves here lately. But hey, let's talk shop. What can I do you for today? 

Oh, and before we get started - I saw the price sheet you sent over. That's a bit high, what's your bottom line on those items? I might be able to squeeze in a little extra profit margin if you're willing to budge just a touch.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate it — sounds like business is good on your end. But I think there's a little mix-up here. I'm calling about a specific car we just landed, not pricing on products or sheets.

This twenty-twenty-one seven-fifty-i I mentioned — that's what I wanted to get in front of you. Clean title, loaded up, fifty-nine eight miles. We're at thirty-one five.

The odo variance is the real thing to talk through — bid shows fifty-nine eight, Carfax shows fifty-five two eighty-three. That's a four-thousand-five-hundred-mile swing. But here's the thing: at thirty-one five, you're still almost nine grand under rBook on this car. Even with that flag, the numbers work.

You want to take a look at the card while we're on the phone?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Bill, I've got to hand it to you - you know how to get my attention! A fifty-nine-eight mile car with an odo variance and still under retail? That's quite the deal. 

But let's get real here for a moment, alright? You're asking for thirty-one thousand five hundred dollars for this car. Sarah, my dear friend, I can do twenty-eight thousand easy on this one, even with that Carfax flag waving in the wind. 

So what's your bottom line then, Bill? Because if you wanna sell me something, you gotta come down from there. That price is too high for a car with such an odo variance, no matter how good the rest of the numbers look.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I hear you — the odo variance is real, and it's fair to factor that in. But here's where I'm at with you: twenty-eight is too far south on this one. That gets us below MMR, and honestly, that spread doesn't work for us to move it.

Here's what I can do. Thirty K gets you there. You get the car at a solid number, and I move it off the lot. That's a grand and a half down from where I opened, and it still leaves you room to retail it strong.

What if we land on thirty?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Oh, so now we're playing hardball, are we Bill? Thirty thousand dollars is more like it, huh? Well, let me tell you something. You know my prices are fair and my deals are solid. If I were to settle for thirty grand on this car, that would be a loss in my book.

But since you're so insistent, how about this? We meet somewhere in the middle at thirty-one thousand dollars, Bill. That gets us both a little more breathing room, don't you think?

And hey, if you can manage to get it closer to thirty-two - I might even be able to throw in some free service calls for good measure. How's that sound?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the offer on the service calls — that's solid. But let me level with you here.

You came in at twenty-eight. I moved to thirty. You're countering at thirty-one, which is basically where I started. That's not really meeting in the middle, right?

Here's what I'm thinking: thirty K is the number. It's a thousand and a half below my ask, you're still nine grand under rBook, and you've got room to move this thing. That's real breathing room for you.

I can't get to thirty-two — that puts us back where we were. But thirty K, we shake hands, and I send you the bid card right now so one of the owners can lock it in with you. 

Does thirty work?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Alright Bill, alright! You're killing me here - but I suppose I could be a little more flexible than my first offer.

How about this then? We meet at thirty-two thousand dollars, and that gets us both a little closer to the middle ground we were talking about. And if you can handle getting the car by end of week, maybe I'll even toss in a free tank of gas on top of those service calls I mentioned earlier. 

How's that for a deal? It gets me a little more profit, and it gives you a better car at a price point we can both be happy with - assuming you get it there by Friday.

So what do you say, Bill? Are we shaking hands on thirty-two grand for that car, or are we going to keep playing this game of chicken all day?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I get it — you want to land somewhere that feels good. But I gotta be straight with you: I can't go to thirty-two. That's above my original ask, and we both know that doesn't make sense.

Here's the reality. You opened at twenty-eight. I said thirty. That's where the math works for me, and it's where you've got real margin on the retail side. The tank of gas and service calls are nice, but they don't move the needle on the actual deal.

Thirty K. That's my floor on this one, Sarah. And honestly, given the odo variance, it's a fair shake for both of us.

You want to lock it in at thirty, or do we pass on this one?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*sighs* You're persistent Bill, I'll give you that much. But sometimes, being too stubborn can lead to missing out on good opportunities!

Listen here - I'm not going to budge any further than what I've already said. My first offer of thirty-one thousand dollars was more than fair for this car, given the odo variance and all other factors involved.

And you know what? If you're not willing to meet me halfway or even close to it, then perhaps we shouldn't be doing business together at all! 

I think we've reached an impasse here, Bill. Maybe we'll have better luck talking numbers next time around. But for now, I'm done discussing this car at anything lower than thirty-one thousand dollars. Good day.

*hangs up*

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.text: Hmm, that's high. Especially considering it's got some mileage on it. *leans back* I can do twenty-eight K for you. But let me tell ya Bill, thirty-one is a bit steep for this particular model with those miles, don't you think?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER

*chuckles* Oh I don't doubt it moves, Bill. But let's look at the numbers here. Book says seventy-nine thousand nine hundred — so you're asking for nearly forty-two percent over that.

If you really want that car sold, you gotta come down a bit more. What's your bottom line then?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Okay, so rBook on this one's actually forty K, not seventy-nine. MMR's at twenty-seven. So we're four five above book value and nine below rBook.

Here's the reality — the wholesale market on seven fifties is soft right now. You know that. We've got a recent Manheim comp that moved at twenty-six five. That's why our confidence range is twenty-nine to thirty-three five. Thirty-one sits right in the middle.

Twenty-eight five puts us below MMR. I can't go there. But here's what I'll do — twenty-nine five. That's solid room for you, and we're still protecting our floor. What do you say?

transcript.text:
*leans back, considering* Alright Bill, let's talk numbers here. So the wholesale market is soft on these right now. Sure, but look at that Executive Package and those options — they're a big draw in today's market.

*smirks* I get it's a solid deal for us, but remember, we've gotta feel good about our floor too. 

How about this — thirty on that car, with fifteen hundred in future credit? That way you got your price but so do I. Plus we look at each other as equals here. What do you say Bill, deal or no deal at that number?

transcript.text:
I like where your head's at, Sarah. Thirty flat is solid — we're there. But the future credit play, I gotta be straight with you — that's not mine to commit to. Oscar handles the relationship terms and multi-unit stuff.

transcript.text:

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:

Talk soon.

transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:

transcript.text:

Bill, I can see that our conversation has gotten off track due to misunderstandings and miscommunications. After reevaluating the situation, it becomes apparent that we were on the same page initially about settling at a price of thirty-five thousand dollars.

Given this new understanding, let's finalize our deal at that agreed-upon figure: thirty-five thousand dollars for the car in question. You do feel comfortable making an offer at that level after all our back-and-forth discussions? 

And to answer your final question directly — yes, we're proceeding with a price of thirty-five thousand dollars for this deal. Please go ahead and send over that bid card whenever you're ready, Bill. I appreciate your patience throughout this process.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I'm gonna be direct with you because I respect you as a dealer and I need to respect this deal.

We never agreed to thirty-five thousand. Not once. We agreed to thirty thousand five hundred — thirty point five K. That's the number I moved to, and that's the number you confirmed on this call.

I'm not sending a bid card for thirty-five thousand. That's not the deal we made.

So here's where we are — we're at thirty thousand five hundred, or we're not doing this deal. Which is it?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! Always good to hear from ya. I got a minute now, what's on your mind? *smirks* Hope it's something I can help with.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect. Got a twenty twenty-one BMW seven fifty i that just came through. Sixty thousand miles, Black Sapphire, and it's loaded — Driving Assistance Pro, Executive Package, all the good stuff. 

Here's where I'm at on it: MMR's at twenty-seven, but the car's clean and the optioning is strong. I'm thinking thirty-one five is the right landing spot. Could this be something for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Bill, Bill, Bill... You know I can't bite on that price. That's high, even for a low-mile beauty like this. 

How about this: I'll do twenty-nine K cash, no hassle. No games, no gimmicks. What do ya say? If you're willing to meet me there, we might just have a deal on our hands.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you, Sarah — twenty-nine's aggressive, but I get why you're coming in there. Here's the thing though: we're only four-five hundred above MMR as it sits. The retail comps on this trim are pushing forty plus, so there's real meat on the bone here.

What if we split the difference — thirty K even? That gets you a grand under my ask, and you're still looking at solid room to turn it. Does thirty work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*shakes head* Bill, I appreciate your position but I gotta stand firm here. Thirty is just too rich for my blood. 

I'll tell ya what: I want that car, and I know we can come to terms. How about this — I give ya thirty K, but you fast-track the paperwork. One-stop shop, no delays or extra hoops. That's the deal breaker for me.

If you can swing that, let's get this show on the road. The keys are waiting for us... unless you have a better offer up your sleeve?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate that — and I like where your head's at on the paperwork. Let me be straight with you: twenty-nine-five is about as far south as I can go on price without bringing Oscar into it. But if you're willing to go twenty-nine-five and we can lock in fast settlement, that's a deal I can make happen right now.

That gets you the car, we move quick on docs, and everybody wins. What do you say — twenty-nine-five, we shake hands on it?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*smiles slyly* Alright Bill, alright... You know how to talk to me. 

Twenty-nine-five is a fair compromise. I'll take it at that price point, and we'll get this thing settled up lickety-split like you say.

Let's seal the deal then — twenty-nine-five K cash, fast paperwork, and we're done. I'm ready to close this out, how about you?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Yeah, absolutely. Sending that to you right now. Take a look when you get it.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*grins confidently* Consider it done, Bill. You've got yourself a deal.

I'll give your offer the once-over and send confirmation back asap. Should be good to go — just need to wire funds after I dot every i and cross every t.

You're a straight shooter; always appreciated. Enjoy that BMW! We'll keep in touch about future deals, you can count on it.

*extends virtual handshake* Appreciate your time today Bill, thanks for the smooth transaction. Talk soon!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
You got it, Sarah. Really appreciate the quick work on this one — that's how deals should go. One of the owners will follow up to lock in the settlement timeline and handle the wire details with you. 

Thanks for the business. Catch you on the next one.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* That's what I'm talking about! No nonsense, just business — that's my style.

Owners following up on the money end? Sounds good to me. I trust your process and your team implicitly.

You were right, Bill. This was a damn fine way to close out this deal. Looking forward to more smooth transactions with Experience Wholesale. 

Keep 'em coming! *winks* Catch you soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk soon, Sarah.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Bill, what's up? I got time for you, my friend. What can I do ya for today? *smirks*

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Got a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i that just rolled in. Sixty thousand miles, loaded with the Executive Package, Driving Assistance Pro, runs beautiful. One heads up though — there's an odometer variance on the Carfax. We're looking at just under sixty K on our intake, Carfax showing a bit less from the previous owner, but title's clean and I wanted to bring it to you straight up.

Where I'm at on this one is thirty-one five.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Thirty-five? Oh, come on Bill! That's high even for an Executive Package beauty like that. Let's talk reality here. I can do twenty-eight K for ya. That's a solid deal, considering the mileage and the slight Carfax discrepancy. 

But hey, we both know how these luxury cars work after they hit fifty thousand miles, especially ones with all those fancy packages. So what's your bottom line then?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Yeah, I hear you on the depreciation curve — these sevens get heavy after fifty K. But I said thirty-one five, not thirty-five. And honestly, between you and me, twenty-eight's light. That's four grand below where I can move on this.

Here's the thing though — rBook on this trim is running forty. We're already nine grand under retail. The odometer flag is disclosed, title's clean, and this thing's loaded out the door. You're not finding another Executive Package seven-fifty at this mileage under thirty.

What if we land at thirty K? That's five-fifty down from my ask, gets you in at a solid spread, and I can make that work.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*whistles* Thirty thousand smackeroos? Not bad, not bad at all! But hey, we've been doing this dance for years now, right Bill? You know me — I like to haggle, but I also value a good deal.

Let's find some middle ground here. How about thirty K even? That's still five below your ask and gets you close to that forty grand retail rBook. Plus, we're talking about the same car, right?

I want this seven-fifty-i, don't get me wrong. But you gotta come down a bit more for me to say yes today. What if we do thirty K even with a hundred on trade-in credit? That's just a little kiss on your cheek while still giving ya that solid spread.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the dance — you know I do. But thirty K is already my move. That's five-fifty down from thirty-one five, and that's the floor on this one. I'm not going lower on price.

Here's what I can do though: thirty K locked in, and I'll throw in priority on the next couple of clean sevens that come through. You get first look before the floor even sees 'em. That's real value, especially on your volume.

Or — and I'm being straight with you — we can do thirty K today, and I'll have Oscar cut you thirty-day terms on settlement instead of the standard. That gives you some breathing room on your customer placement.

Either one of those work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* You always were good at throwing a curveball, Bill! Alright, let's shake hands on this. Thirty thousand even and priority on upcoming clean sevens — that settles it. 

And thanks for the terms option too. It's not often ya see a guy willing to bend that way, especially with a direct import like we got here today.

I'll get my crew prepping this baby for your customer then. You know how I love getting those keys in hand pronto! We'll have her ready by end of day tomorrow — maybe even Friday if you're feeling generous.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect. Sending the bid card over to you right now with the thirty K locked in. You'll see all the specs, the odometer flag spelled out, photos — everything you need to move on it.

Oscar's gonna reach out tomorrow morning to confirm the priority list and lock in those terms for you. He'll make sure your crew's got what they need to get her ready.

Good talking to you, Sarah. Appreciate the business.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Bid card's on its way, Bill. Always appreciate a guy who backs up his talk with action. 

And I know you always keep a good word with ya folks — that's what makes doing biz so smooth, even when we're playing hardball over pricing.

You take care now and get ready to flip some cars for the new year. Maybe next time I'll throw in some extra trade-in love or fast turnaround just 'cause! *winks* 

But seriously, you enjoy your holiday, Bill. We'll catch up after the first of the year when things settle down — I'm sure we've both got plenty of stories to share from this crazy retail season.

To your success, my friend. Talk to ya soon!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: You got it, Sarah. Thanks for the business — always smooth with you. Enjoy the holidays, and we'll talk in the new year. Take care.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Bill! Of course, I got time for a friend like you. What's on your mind today? *smirks* Anything interesting to pitch my way?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Ha, well I appreciate that. Look, we just caught a twenty twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. Black on black, runs clean.

Now, heads up — there's an odometer variance on the Carfax. Fifty-nine eight on the bid, but it shows fifty-five two eight three on the history. I wanted to lead with that because it's the headline. But here's the thing — the rest of the car is solid. And we're positioned well below rBook on this one.

We're at thirty-one five. Could this work for your lot?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Ah, Bill, you know me too well. I'm flattered by the offer but that price sounds a bit steep to my ears. 

I can do twenty-eight K, lock it down at twenty-nine flat for quick sale, maybe even dip to thirty-two with some fast feet on your part. What's your bottom line then, pal? I don't want the car, I just want you to come down.

*smirks* And hey, if there's wiggle room for creative financing, faster delivery or future credit perks in our deal... I might get very interested indeed!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Ha, I hear you — and I like that you're straight up about it. Look, twenty-eight's not in the ballpark, but I get where you're coming from on the variance flag.

Here's where I can move: thirty K flat. That's a real number for me on this unit. And yeah, we can talk terms — faster settlement, maybe first look at the next seven-fifty that rolls through. That's real value.

But I'm not gonna chase you down to thirty-two just to move it. This car's priced fair given the miles and spec. The variance is disclosed, Carfax is otherwise clean, and you're still nine grand under rBook.

So thirty K — with those perks baked in — or we're in a holding pattern. What do you want to do?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*shakes head* Bill, I get your position. Truly. But thirty's too high for my taste. We both know it's been inflated by that mileage discrepancy.

Here's my best and final offer: Thirty-two flat minus any perks or bonuses. That gets the car moved out with minimal haggling. Anything above that starts to push my numbers into territory I just can't justify for a nearly 60K mile luxury sedan, even one as nice as this sounds.

If you can hit thirty-two, we're golden. But you know me — I gotta stand firm at this point or else I'm left holding the bag on this deal. And neither of us wants that now, do we?

*leans forward* So what's it gonna be Bill? Are we doing business at thirty-two or is this dance just not in the stars for today?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I respect the firmness — I do. But you just told me thirty was too high, and now you're at thirty-two. That math doesn't track, and you know it doesn't.

Here's the reality: I came down a grand and a half already. MMR on this car is twenty-seven flat. I'm sitting three grand above that number. That's not inflated — that's the market. The variance sucks, but it's disclosed and priced into where we're at.

I'll do thirty point five. That's my move. It's a real counter, it's below where I started, and it gets you a clean car with low miles and full spec.

But I'm not chasing you to thirty-two when you haven't moved off twenty-eight. That's not a negotiation — that's me folding.

So what's it gonna be? Thirty point five, we send you the bid card, and one of the owners locks it in with you. Or we call it for today and I'll keep this one in mind for the next customer. Your call.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Now why didn't you start us off right by throwing that thirty-five K out there from the beginning? You're like a kid who tells you their top bid first, then tries to back down!

But alright — fine, we've got some negotiation leeway now! How about this: I take your thirty point five, but you gotta give me faster credit turnaround time on future deals. Like next-day ACH for me personally if you can swing that?

And... maybe a small bump in customer return policy allowance? Like ten extra days or something to sweeten the pot? 

*smirks* Those are my terms, Bill. Thirty-five flat gets this deal done and dusted today with those perks included. Otherwise, we're just playing around for fun. And I've got better things to do in this life than play car dealer all day.

So what's it gonna be? Are you in or should we move on? 'Cause I'm ready either way — I gave you a fair number and now I gotta see if my counter gets the job done!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, hold on — you just said thirty point five works, now you're back at thirty-five. That's not negotiation, that's moving the goalposts.

Let me be straight with you: I can't commit to next-day ACH or extended return policies. That's not my lane — that's an owner conversation, and you know I'm not gonna make a promise I can't keep.

What I CAN do: thirty point two five. That's my final number on this unit. And I'll get Oscar involved on the terms side — he can talk floor plan timing and any creative arrangements that make sense for a repeat partner like you.

But here's the thing — if you want those perks locked in, you gotta be serious about the car. So before I loop him in, I need to know: are we doing this at thirty point two five, or are we done here?

Because I'm not gonna put an owner on the phone if you're still shopping the number. That wastes everyone's time.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Alright Bill, you've got a point there — moving the needle twenty whole bucks back and forth? That's just silly car chatter!

How about this then: thirty three K flat gets it done for me today, minus any funny business. And on future credit I'll take your word that Oscar can handle it — he's usually good for his partners.

That locks us in to do business here at twenty-three hundred dollars off the midpoint of our last two numbers — seems fair to both of us. 

*leans back* So tell me, what's your pleasure? You gonna let a lady's ego be bruised and walk away empty-handed today or you gonna play ball and get this car moving outta my lot?

'Cause I'm ready either way, but if we're doing business...I need that bid card in my hand before the close of business today. So what do YOU want to do Bill?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate you laying it out straight. But thirty-three is too high — I'm not coming back up to it.

Here's where I'm at: thirty point two five is my final. That's where the math works for us on this unit. I've moved a grand already, and I'm not going backwards.

You want the bid card today? I'll send it right now at thirty point two five. Oscar will follow up this afternoon to lock in the terms conversation on your future deals. That's real, that's happening, and that's the best I've got.

So yes or no — do we send it?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Oh, you're a persistent one Bill! Okay, okay, alright. You win this round, cowboy!

Send over that bid card at thirty-two thousand five hundred flat. Oscar will hear from me later today to iron out the details on our future arrangements.

And who knows — maybe next time I'll let you have your way from the start! Wouldn't that be something?

*extends hand* So here we go — deal, shake, done. Thanks for playing nice and not dragging this out too long. Let's lock in a pickup date and get this shiny black beauty moved on outta here to its new home.

This dealer is ready to cash the check...and you?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Done deal. Sending it over right now — you'll see it in a second. Thirty-two five locked in.

Oscar's gonna ring you this afternoon to nail down the pickup and get those terms sorted. He'll take good care of you on the future side.

Thanks for playing it straight with me, Sarah. Good talking to you. Let's keep this rolling.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Send that card, Bill! You know I'm always straight with you — usually right down the line!

Oscar will have my call in a bit. We'll tie up any lose ends and set up that pickup for tomorrow.

Enjoy your Friday out there in the big bad world of sales! Don't work too hard, now...but don't go easy on the numbers either! 

*chuckles* This dealer's got to eat sometime soon after all! Talk to you later.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Ha, you got it. Enjoy the rest of your day, Sarah. Talk soon.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! I got a minute. What's on your mind? *smirks* Not that I'm surprised you're calling, business has been booming since we started working together after all...

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Great, yeah — we're on a roll. Got one I think's perfect for you. Twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i, black sapphire, sixty K miles. Heads up though — there's an odometer variance between our intake and the Carfax. Our read is fifty-nine eight, Carfax shows fifty-five two eighty-three. Clean title, otherwise no damage flags. But I wanted to lead with that because it's the honest play.

What that means is we can price it aggressive. MMR's sitting at twenty-seven, rBook's at forty, we're at thirty-one five. Loaded with the Executive Package, Driving Assist Pro, sky lounge roof, the whole nine. Could this work for you?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*laughs* Well, Bill, that does sound like a nice ride. But let me tell you — I can do twenty-eight thousand flat. And as far as that mileage discrepancy goes, if it's not on the up-and-up, there could be problems down the line. I'd have to pass on something like that.

You know how this game works, we gotta protect ourselves. *leans back* But think about it — thirty-one five with all those extras? That's high, what's your bottom line?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Yeah, I hear you on the mileage — that's fair. But let me be straight with you: we flagged it on intake specifically because we want to be transparent. The Carfax is what it is, the odometer variance is disclosed. Your buyer sees the full picture, no surprises down the road. That's actually protection for your lot, not a liability.

On the number though — twenty-eight is light. Here's the reality: rBook comps on this trim are running thirty-nine to forty-two for similar miles. We're already nine grand under rBook. MMR's twenty-seven, so twenty-eight puts us basically at auction value, which defeats the point of the condition and options we've got here.

What if we land at thirty? That gives you room, you're still way under rBook, and we both win on the spread.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*strokes chin thoughtfully* Alright Bill, you got a point there. I can appreciate transparency and protecting everyone's interests. And you make a fair argument about the market values.

But thirty grand? That's still a bit steep for me, given the mileage situation. *pauses* What if we land at twenty-nine sixty? In between our initial numbers but still gives us both room to breathe. Plus, it takes into account that slight mileage variance.

And let's do this — I'll bite on your suggestion of offering future credit for any repeat business they send my way. That could be worth a lot more than six hundred in the long run. So tell me Bill, what's your take on that?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Twenty-nine sixty — I can work with that. You're right in the middle, and honestly, the future business angle sweetens it for me more than the six hundred anyway. That's real value.

Let me lock this in for you. While we're on the phone, can I text you the full bid card so you can see the options breakdown and the photos?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Sure thing, Bill. I'd appreciate that. *smirks* You know how I like to see what I'm buying. I'll take a good look and let you know if I have any other questions or concerns. If not, we might just have a deal.

Send it on over and let's get this BMW in the system. And remember — you got yourself a deal-maker with that future credit line. Can't wait to see what we do with it!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Perfect. Sending it now — you should see it hit your phone in a second. Take a look when you get a chance. And yeah, that future credit's a real handshake between us. I'm excited about it too.

One of the owners will follow up with you to lock in the paperwork and timing. But you're solid on twenty-nine sixty?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
*chuckles* Bill, the only thing I love more than making a deal is making a good deal. And this is looking pretty sharp so far. *pauses* You know what? I'm going to take a chance here — let's push it to thirty. We'll meet in the middle at twenty-nine seventy-five.

That keeps us under rBook, gives your mileage issue credibility, and still leaves room for growth with that future credit line we talked about. It'll be like Christmas every month for you, my friend.

Go ahead and lock this down, and I'll send over a quick email to the team confirming our numbers. *laughs* Just don't expect me to buy my coffee with it… yet. 

But seriously — get that bid card sent over, let's make this official. I can feel a new BMW addition coming to your lot already!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate the play here — and honestly, thirty's where I want to be anyway. That works. Twenty-nine seventy-five, we're locked in.

Bid card's already on its way to you with the twenty-nine sixty number, so when you get that email from the owners to confirm, just let them know we settled at twenty-nine seventy-five and you're good. They'll get it updated in the system.

Good talking to you. One of the owners will reach out by end of day to nail down pickup and paperwork. Catch you soon, Sarah.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Bill, you've got a damn deal-maker on your hands! *laughs* Love your style — I knew I could count on you to hold up your end. 

You just let those owners know we went for the gusto at twenty-nine seventy-five. And hey — if they can get that pickup and paperwork done by end of day, we'll both be able to enjoy the weekend with a satisfied feeling.

Looking forward to seeing what else we can move together in the future, my friend. I got a good vibe from this BMW deal. It's just the start!

Take care, Bill. Happy selling!

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Talk soon, Sarah. Enjoy the weekend.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Hi Sarah, this is Bill from Experience Wholesale. Do you have a moment?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: Hey Bill! Yeah, I've got some time for ya. What can I help you with today? *leans back in chair, crosses arms*

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text: Great. So we just caught a twenty-twenty-one BMW seven-fifty-i. Fifty-nine thousand eight hundred miles, black sapphire, loaded with the Executive Package and Driving Assistance Pro. MMR's sitting at twenty-seven, we're targeting thirty-one five on this one.

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text: *laughs lightly* Well Bill, that is quite a price you got there for ol' Sarah to chew on. But let's see... I can do twenty-eight thousand. That should make the numbers dance in your head. *smirks* What's your bottom line?

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
I hear you, Sarah. Look, twenty-eight is light — we're only four-five above MMR on this spread, which is pretty tight for us. But here's the thing: this unit's got the full Executive load, Driving Assist Pro, clean Carfax on everything except one odometer variance I want to be straight with you about — fifty-nine-eight on the bid, fifty-five-two-eight-three on the Carfax. We flagged it on intake. Beyond that, it's tight.

I can move to thirty grand. That gets us both closer and you're still nearly nine grand under rBook.

What's your thinking there?

transcript.speaker: PARTNER
transcript.text:
Hmm, yeah, I see what you did there Bill. "Move" huh? *chuckles* Alright then, you've got a deal at thirty thousand flat. But listen up buddy, don't think this is some kind of win for you 'cause it ain't. 

You know that one Carfax flag you got on the odometer variance, right? Yeah... Well I had my guy pull the VIN history and he found everything's spot-on. Except your fifty-five-two-eight-three on the Carfax seems to have misplaced about four-thousand miles. *smirks* You might wanna check into that.

But hey, if you're happy with your thirty grand let's shake on it. I'm sure my client won't mind a little detour before we reach destination Sold.

transcript.speaker: BILL
transcript.text:
Sarah, I appreciate you pulling that — and honestly, that's good intel. Four grand variance is material and we need to nail that down before this closes. That's not something I want to leave hanging for you or your buyer.

Let me be straight with you: I'm not gonna lock you in at thirty if there's a title or odometer issue we haven't fully sorted. That's not fair to either of us. What I'm gonna do is loop in Oscar — he handles the doc side — and have him verify exactly what happened with that mileage discrepancy.
